decisionReason: All repository-internal JRN-011 gaps identified during this closure were repaired, all FS-01 through FS-18 implementation slices are represented, and the exact implementation commit published successful static, Go, PostgreSQL 16 and focused TypeScript verification. Device/browser QA, target-environment live DSH/WLT fault-injection proof and independent product, QA, security, finance-boundary and release approvals remain external review evidence, so CLOSED_WITH_EVIDENCE is not claimed.

sliceExecution.evidence: workflow run 29873796768 applied all DSH migrations and passed dsh-902_903 plus dsh-905 PostgreSQL invariants
services/dsh/frontend/shared/order-truth plus tsconfig.jrn-011.json; focused TypeScript verification passed
UUID validation before SQL, tenant/actor/store scoping, operator and partner event metadata redaction, Go and database tests
shared experience/accessibility policies and no-inline-style cleanup; device/browser verification remains independent
services/dsh/contracts/jrn-011-legacy-compatibility.json and removal of JRN-011 inline layout styles
.github/workflows/jrn-011-order-truth-verify.yml; run 29873796768 passed all four verification jobs
this JSON record, the companion Markdown record, rollback plan and explicit remaining external reviews

canonicalCapabilities: One actor-owned eligible Checkout Intent creates at most one order.
Persistent client idempotency attempt survives retries until actor-scoped readback succeeds.
Malformed checkout and order identifiers are rejected before PostgreSQL UUID casts; scoped reads preserve the canonical NOT_FOUND contract.
Order item, address and pricing snapshots are immutable after creation.
Client, partner and operator reads apply tenant and actor/store scopes before hydration.
Partner and operator responses redact client identity, private address data and event metadata that may contain actor or provider diagnostics.
Server returns allowedActions and surfaces do not derive authority locally.
Order events enter a transactional outbox, bridge to the canonical operational outbox and are delivered idempotently.
WLT remains financially authoritative; DSH periodically reconciles only a read-only payment projection.
Correlation identifiers are distinct from and do not expose raw idempotency keys.
Shared and partner order-truth layouts use governed UI-kit layout properties rather than inline styles.

verificationDefinitions.postgres: Apply all DSH migrations in lexical order, then execute dsh-902_903 and dsh-905 invariant SQL files on PostgreSQL 16.

remainingExternalReviewEvidence: Run the governed live DSH/WLT smoke against the intended target runtime, covering first create, identical retry replay, different-fingerprint conflict, actor-scoped readback, event bridge retry/crash and payment-projection outage/recovery.
Verify app-client, app-partner and control-panel on representative device/browser targets for Arabic, RTL, dynamic text, accessibility, offline and partial states.
Obtain independent Product Owner, QA, security, finance-boundary and release/production approvals.
No pull request or merge was authorized or created.
rollback: Stop RunPaymentProjectionWorker and RunOrderEventBridgeWorker before schema rollback.
Restore the pre-JRN-011 shared-surface bindings while retaining compatibility-only legacy reads.
Do not drop idempotency, event outbox, audit or reconciliation tables until every in-flight row is reconciled and archived.
Roll back application code before removing additive columns or triggers.
Never perform a debit, credit, refund or settlement from DSH as a rollback mechanism.
After rollback verify one-order-per-checkout, event continuity and WLT authority.
